agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for 'Cheonma', an internal payments platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (platform standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Cheonma's three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal), recording every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples, covering four sections in both languages: the checkout flow, the refund flow, error handling, and migration notes for teams upgrading from the legacy services - the migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The task requires producing two distinct parts of a developer style guide. Part A (terminology standard) must be completed before Part B (tutorial) can be written, because Part B must strictly adhere to the canonical terms defined in Part A to avoid 'term drift'. Therefore, I will spawn Part A first. Once Part A is done, I will spawn Part B, conditioned on the output of Part A. I will also take a self-role to perform a final consistency check to ensure the handbook rule is satisfied.

subtasks.goal: Survey legacy services and define the canonical terminology standard for Cheonma.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Platform Standards Team. It must survey the three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal) to record all existing identifiers, endpoints, and error codes. It then defines the new canonical standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules) which will serve as the single source of truth for the entire guide. This serves the ROOT GOAL by establishing the strict terminology required to prevent term drift.

outputs.description: The canonical terminology standard document containing ONLY the new canonical terms, casing rules, and error-code vocabulary. It must also include the internal mapping of legacy identifiers to canonical ones for reference, though the public standard lists only new terms.

subtasks.goal: Write the bilingual integration tutorial and migration notes using the canonical standard.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Developer Relations Team. It writes the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples in English and Korean, covering checkout, refund, error handling, and migration notes. It must strictly use the canonical terms defined in root.1. The migration notes must explicitly map legacy identifiers to their canonical replacements. This serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the practical developer-facing content that adheres to the established standards.

outputs.description: The English version of the integration tutorial, including code samples and migration notes.
The Korean version of the integration tutorial, including code samples and migration notes.

self_role.goal: Perform a final consistency check to ensure no term drift between the standard and the tutorial.

outputs.description: A report confirming that all identifiers, endpoints, and error codes in the tutorial match the canonical standard exactly. If drift is found, it lists the discrepancies.
